core_mechanism: a critical-point scaling limit in which discrete lattice observables that satisfy a discrete complex-analyticity / discrete Cauchy-Riemann relation converge to conformally invariant continuous holomorphic fields, with the limit independent of the microscopic lattice (universality)
brief_reason: The abstract's whole method rests on introducing discrete holomorphic observables at criticality on a large family of planar graphs and showing their scaling limits are universal and conformally invariant, i.e. lattice-detail-independent continuum holomorphic fields.
